questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform ring of mass $m$ is placed on a rough horizontal fixed surface as shown in the figure. The coefficient of friction between the left part of the ring and left part of the horizontal surface is $\mu_1=0.6 \pi$ and between right half and the surface is $\mu_2=0.2 \pi$. At the instant shown, now the ring has been imparted an angular velocity in clockwise sense in the figure shown. At this moment magnitude of acceleration of centre $O$ of the ring (in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^2$) is (take $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$)
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 2\left[\int_0^{\pi / 2}\left(\mu_1-\mu_2\right) R g \lambda \cos \theta d \theta\right]=2\left(\mu_1-\mu_2\right) R \lambda g \\ & \therefore a=\frac{2\left(\mu_1-\mu_2\right) R \lambda g}{2 \pi R \lambda}=4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A flexible drive belt runs over a frictionless pulley as shown in figure. The pulley is rotating freely about the vertical axis passing through the centre O of the pulley. The vertical axis is fixed on the horizontal smooth surface. The mass per unit length of the drive belt is $1 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}$ and the tension in the drive belt 8 N. The speed of the drive belt is $2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Find the net normal force applied by the belt on the pulley in newton.
questions.solution: $2 T \sin \frac{d \theta}{2}-d N=\lambda R d \theta \frac{v^2}{R}$ ($\lambda$ is linear mass density of belt) $\therefore T d \theta-d N=\lambda v^2 d \theta$ $\therefore \text { so total normal }=\int_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{+\frac{\pi}{2}} \mathrm{dN} \cos \theta=\int_{\frac{\pi}{2}}^{+\frac{\pi}{2}} T \cos \theta d \theta-\lambda \mathrm{v}^2 \int_{-\frac{\pi}{2}}^{+\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos \theta \mathrm{~d} \theta$ $\therefore \mathrm{N}=8$ newton
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the system shown in the figure, a bead of mass m can slide on the string. There is friction between the bead and the string. Block $B$ has mass equal to twice that of the bead. The system is released from rest with length $\ell=18.75 \mathrm{~m}$ of the string hanging below the bead. Assuming the pulley and string to be massless. Find the distance (in meter) moved by the block B before the bead slips out of the thread.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{mg}-\mathrm{T}=m \mathrm{a}_1 \\ & 2 \mathrm{mg}-2 \mathrm{~T}=2 \mathrm{ma}_2 \end{aligned}$ So, $a_1=a_2$ Relative acceleration of bead with respect to end $=3 \mathrm{a}$ $\therefore$ displacement of block $x=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{at}^2=\frac{\ell}{3}=6.25 \mathrm{~m}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An old record player of 10 cm radius turns at $10 ~\mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$ while mounted on a $30^{\circ}$ incline as shown in the figure. A particle of mass m can be placed anywhere on the rotating record. If the least possible coefficient of friction $\mu$ that must exist for no slipping to occur is $\mu$, find $2 \sqrt{3} \mu$. [Take $\left.\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A block of mass $m$ is released on a smooth inclined wedge of inclination $30^{\circ}$ and mass $M$. Height of the block (from ground) varies with time as $h=1.5-1.5 t^2$. ($h$ in metre and $t=$ time in second). The acceleration of M is $\frac{x}{\sqrt{3}} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$. Find the value of x :
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{x}}$ and $\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{y}}$ component of acceleration of m and a acceleration of M .
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{a_y}{a+a_x}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \\ & a+a_x=\sqrt{3} a_y=3 \sqrt{3} \\ & m g-N \cos 30^{\circ}=ma_y \\ & N \sin 30^{\circ}= ma_x \end{aligned}$ $\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{y}}=\left|\frac{d^2 h}{d t^2}\right|=3$ on solving $\begin{aligned} a & =\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \\ \therefore \quad x & =2 \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A stick of mass density $\lambda=8 \mathrm{~kg} / \mathrm{m}$ rests on a disc of radius $\mathrm{R}=$ 20 cm as shown in the figure. The stick makes an angle $\theta=37^{\circ}$ with the horizontal and is tangent to the disc at its upper end. Friction exists at all points of contact and assume that it is large enough to keep the system at rest. Find the friction force (in Newton) between the ground and the disc. (take $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \tau_A=0 \\
& N=\frac{M g}{2} \cos \theta
\end{aligned}$

For disc, $N \sin \theta=f+f \cos \theta$
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore f=\frac{N \sin \theta}{1+\cos \theta}=\frac{M g \sin \theta \cos \theta}{2(1+\cos \theta)} \\
& M=\lambda L \text { and } L=\frac{R}{\tan \frac{\theta}{2}} \\
& f=\frac{1}{2} \lambda g R \cos \theta \\
& =\frac{1}{2} \times 8 \times 10 \times \frac{2}{10} \times \frac{4}{5} \\
& =\frac{32}{5}=6.40 \mathrm{~N}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The wedge shown in the figure can slide without friction on a horizontal tabletop. The mass of the wedge is M and its angle of elevation is $a=30^{\circ}$. A body of mass $m$ slides down without friction along the wedge, the path of the body makes an angle of $\varphi=60^{\circ}$ with the ground. Find the ratio of the masses, $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{M}$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & a_1 \cos \left[90^{\circ}-(\phi-\alpha)\right]=a_2 \sin \alpha \\ & a_1 \sin (\phi-\alpha)=a_2 \sin \alpha \end{aligned}...(i)$ Conservation has no acceleration x direction $\Rightarrow M a_2=m a_1 \sin \phi...(ii)$ From (i) and (ii) $\Rightarrow \frac{m}{M} \sin \phi=\frac{\sin (\phi-\alpha)}{\sin \alpha}$ $\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{M}}=\frac{\tan \phi-\tan \alpha}{\tan \alpha}$ $\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{M}}=\frac{\tan 60-\tan 30}{\tan 30}=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A spool, initially at rest, is kept on a frictionless incline making an angle $\theta=37^{\circ}$ with the horizontal. The mass of the spool is 2 kg and it is pulled by a string as shown with a force of $\mathrm{T}=10 \mathrm{~N}$. The string connecting the spool and the pulley is initially horizontal. Find the initial acceleration of the spool on the incline. Express your answer in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^2$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A transparent lift A is going upwards with velocity $20 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ and retarding at the rate of $8 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$. Second transparent lift B is located in front of it and is going down at $10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ with retardation of $2 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$. At the same instant a bolt from the ceiling of lift A drops inside lift A. If height of car of lift A is 16 m then find the distance travelled by bolt as observed by a person in lift B till the time it collides with floor of lift A.
questions.solution: In the ref. frame of A : $\begin{array}{ll} \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{b} / \mathrm{A}}=0 & \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{b} / \mathrm{A}}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2 \\ \mathrm{~S}_{\mathrm{b} / \mathrm{A}}=16 \mathrm{~m}- & \\ \mathrm{T}=\sqrt{\left(\frac{2 \times 16}{2}\right)}=4 \mathrm{~s} & \text { for Bolt (b) }: \\ \mathrm{t}_1=\frac{30}{12}=\frac{10}{4}=2.5 \mathrm{~s} \Rightarrow & 0=30-12\left(\mathrm{t}_1\right) \\ \mathrm{h}_1=\frac{75}{2} & \mathrm{~h}_1=\frac{(30)^2}{2(12)}=\frac{900}{24} \end{array}$ In the ref. frame of $B$ : $\begin{array}{lll} \mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{b} / \mathrm{B}}=30 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}- & \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{b} / \mathrm{B}}=12 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2 & \mathrm{~T}=4 \mathrm{~s} \\ \mathrm{~V}_{\mathrm{A} / \mathrm{B}}=30 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}- & \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{A} / \mathrm{B}}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2 & \end{array}$ for $A$ : $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{A} / \mathrm{B}}=30(4)-\frac{1}{2}(10)(4)^2=120-80=40$ $\text { distance }=2 \mathrm{~h}_1+\mathrm{h}-\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{A} / \mathrm{B}}=75+16-40=51 \mathrm{~m}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A disc of mass $m$ and radius $R$ is placed over a plank of same mass m . There is sufficient friction between the disc and the plank to prevent slipping. A force F is applied at the centre of the disc. Then, the Acceleration of the plank is $\frac{\mathrm{F}}{\mathrm{Km}}$. Find the value of K .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the shown figure $m_A, m_B$ and $m_C$ are the masses of the three blocks, the incline is frictionless $m_A=5 \mathrm{~kg}, m_B=10 \mathrm{~kg}$, $m_C=2 \mathrm{~kg}$. coefficient of friction between $A$ and $B$ is 0.5 and between $B$ and $C$ is 0.1 . The frictional force on block $A$ is $6 K$ Newton. Find the value of K. (Take $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & m_{A g} \sin \theta=5 \times 10 \times \sin 37^{\circ}=5 \times 10 \times \frac{3}{5}=30 \mathrm{~N} \\ & f_{\max }=\mu_{A B}\left(m_c+m_B\right) g \cos \theta=48 \mathrm{~N} \\ & \text { hence } f=30 \mathrm{~N}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Atwood's machine was an apparatus that allowed a direct verification of Newton's second law. It could also be used to measure g. Two equal blocks of mass $M$ hang at either side of a pulley; see figure. A small square rider of mass $m$ is placed on one block. When the block is released, it accelerates for a distance H till the rider is caught by a ring that allows the block to pass. From then on the system moves at constant speed which is measured by timing the fall through a distance $D$. $t$ is the time moved at constant speed. Find the value of g . [Given : $M=4 \mathrm{~kg}, \mathrm{~m}=1 \mathrm{~kg}, \mathrm{D}=2 \mathrm{~m}, \mathrm{H}=2 \mathrm{~m}, \mathrm{t}=1 \mathrm{~s}$ ]
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: In the figure shown a plank of length $L=16 \mathrm{~m}$, mass $\mathrm{m}_2=5 \mathrm{~kg}$ rests on a smooth surface. Upper surface of plank is rough with coefficient of kinetic friction $\mu_{\mathrm{k}}=0.5$ and static friction $\mu_{\mathrm{s}}=0.6$. A small block of mass $m_1=2 \mathrm{~kg}$ is placed over it. A force F of magnitude 30 N is applied on block $\mathrm{m}_1$. What is displacement of plank (in m ) till the small block falls over from plank ?
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A board is being moved with a cosntant velocity $\mathrm{v}_0$ on a smooth horizontal plane. A small block is projected on the rough boards. Coefficient of friction between board and block is $\mu=0.3$. Block is projected with velocity $\mathrm{v}_1=25 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ at an angle of $\theta=37^{\circ}$ relative to ground. What is the velocity of block ( $\mathrm{in} \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ ) relative to ground after time $\mathrm{t}=10 \mathrm{sec}$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & a=-3 \\ & u=\sqrt{\left(25 \times \frac{4}{5}-8\right)^2+\left(25 \times \frac{3}{5}\right)^2}=\sqrt{(12)^2+(15)^2}=3 \sqrt{41}\end{aligned}$
at $\mathrm{t}=\frac{3 \sqrt{41}}{3}$ so it will stop relative motion before 10 sec . So it will have same velocity as plank.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A block of mass $m=1 \mathrm{~kg}$ is connected with an ideal string as shown in figure. If the string is parallel to incline at the instant of release, find the ratio of acceleration of $m$ to that of $M=2 \mathrm{~kg}$ instanteously. Consider all the surface to be smooth.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The coefficient of friction between the two blocks shown in the figure is μ=0.4 and between the lower block and the ground is zero. The blocks are given velocities of 2 m s-1 and 8 m s-1 respectively in the direction shown in the figure. In how much time (in seconds) the slipping between the blocks will stop?
questions.solution:
The common velocity of the blocks can be calculated as follows

3v=1×2+2×8

v=6 m s-1

The acceleration of the 1 kg block is

a=μg=4 m s-2

v=2+4t

⇒t=1 s
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A 1 kg block moving on horizontal rough surface of friction coefficient 0.6 is pushed with a force varying with time $t$ in seconds as shown in figure. If initial velocity of block was $4.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Find the velocity (in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ ) at $\mathrm{t}=3 \mathrm{~s}$.
questions.solution:
$f_k=6 \mathrm{~N}$ which will act till velocity is not zero.
Let velocity becomes zero at t , then $0-4.5=\int_0^t(3-3 t) d t \Rightarrow t=3$ so at $\mathrm{t}=3, \mathrm{v}=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
